content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                    322-5901           322-6441           327-6444

December 30, 1977

77-12-070

Nelson Richardson
Richardson Art
1920 Williams Street
Eureka, Ca. 95501

Dear Mr. Richardson:

     This is in response to your letter to the Commission
dated November 28, 1977, concerning a potential conflict of
interest situation involving your firm's contract with Humboldt
County to refurbish the window display exhibit in the Capitol
Building in Sacramento. I trust the following informal advice
issued pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b), will enable
you to resolve the problem.

     The basic conflict of interest prohibitions in the
Political Reform Act are found in Government Code Section 87100
which provides:

     No public official at any level of state or
     local government shall make, participate in
     making or in any way attempt to use his official
     position to influence a governmental decision
     in which he knows or has reason to know he has
     a financial interest.

     In order to be subject to these prohibitions one must
be a public official. Government Code Section 82048 defines
public official as "every member, officer, employee or con-
sultant of a state or local government agency." Commission
regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1) further
defines "member" as follows:

     "Member" shall include, but not be limited to,
     salaried or unsalaried members of boards or
     commissions with decision-making authority. A
     board or commission possesses decision-making
     authority whenever:

Nelson Richardson
December 30, 1977
Page Two

(A) It may make a final governmental decision;

(B) It may compel a governmental decision;
or it may prevent a governmental decision
either by reason of an exclusive power to
initiate the decision or by reason of a veto
which may not be overridden; or

(C) It makes substantive recommendations
which are, and over an extended period of
time have been, regularly approved without
significant amendment or modification by
another public official or governmental agency.

After consulting with the County Counsel for Humboldt
County and analyzing the duties and responsibilities of the
Tourism Development Advisory Board, we have concluded that
that body is not "decision-making" within the meaning of the
regulation. Moreover, based on the facts provided to us, we
do not think that you are an "officer, employee, or consultant"
of the county by virtue of your participation on such advisory
board.1/

Accordingly, we conclude that you are not a "public
official" within the meaning of Government Code Section 87100
by virtue of your membership on the Tourism Development Advisory
Board. Therefore, the strictures of the Political Reform Act
do not reach your participation in that organization's deliberations.

You will note that Government Code Section 87100 prohibits the public acts of those persons included within its
coverage. It does not prohibit persons from engaging in any
private financial activities, but rather circumscribes their
conduct in public office assuming such private relationships
exist. However, you should be aware that Government Code
Sections 1090, et seq. prohibit the making of certain contracts
in which public officers may have a financial interest. The
Commission is without authority to interpret that provision
and declines to do so in the instant case. With respect to
questions you may have concerning that section we would suggest
you consult with the County Counsel or the Office of the Attorney General, Government Law Section.

________
1/
Additional support for this conclusion with respect
to the scope of coverage of the Act is found in 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 82019(c) which provides:

..."Designated employee" does not include...
any unsalaried member of any board or commission
which serves a solely advisory function.

Nelson Richardson
December 30, 1977
Page Three


     Thank you for writing the Commission.  If I can be
of further assistance in this or any other matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflict of Interest Division

November 28, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

At the request of the County Administrative Officer
for Humboldt County, I am submitting the enclosed
series of letters. These letters reflect the circumstances and events related to a design proposal
submitted by me to the County of Humboldt. It is
the County Administrative Officer's desire that you
make a ruling on this particular situation before
the County directs me to proceed in designing the
State Capitol Building display.

Your consideration of this matter is prompted by
the fact that I am an appointed member of the
Humboldt County Tourism Advisory Board which recommended to the Board of Supervisors that I be
considered for this design assignment. The recommendation was based on my professional qualifications and the fact that I had initially been
requested by the Supervisors to make recommendations on our county's deteriorating Capitol display.

My recommendations were reviewed by the Board of
Supervisors and the Tourism Development Advisory
Board. The Board of Supervisors then directed the
County Administrative Officer to request a formal
proposal from me. The proposal to redesign the
Capitol exhibit was accepted and now we would like
your board to rule on a possible conflict of interest
before we enter into a contract.

If any additional information is needed, please
contact me. We look forward to your ruling on this
matter.

Very truly yours,

Nelson Richardson

Enclosures

CC: Mr. Ron Holden, C.A.O., Humboldt County

September 2, 1977


Mr. Ron Holden
County Administrative Officer
825 Fifth Street
Eureka, California  95501

Dear Mr. Holden:

In response to the Board of Supervisors recent directive, I am pleased to submit a proposal to design and construct a new exhibit representing Humboldt County in the State Capitol Building.

I propose to design and construct a colorful three-dimensional display that will emphasize our County's appeal to the tourist. The exciting exhibit will stand out from other county exhibits and attract the vacationer to our area. Although dynamic in its format, the display will require no maintenance which has been a problem in the past. The content of the exhibit will be in keeping with contemporary design standards and should serve as an outstanding example of Humboldt County's offerings.

The design sequence would include a preliminary input meeting with those interested supervisors. Upon concept approval, fabrication would commence. The exhibit could be installed eight to ten weeks after contract approval. The estimated cost for design, construction, delivery and installation in Sacramento would be $1,425.00.

My professional abilities are reflected by a Masters Degree in Art and eight years of graphic communication experience. Many local business recommendations can be provided at your request. My clients have included Winzler & Kelly, Consulting Engineers, Simpson Timber Company, Eureka Fisheries and the Ingomar Club.

I look forward to serving the County in this design capacity.

Very truly yours,


Nelson Richardson

August 11, 1977


Board of Supervisors
825 Fifth Street
Eureka, CA 95501

Supervisors,

Regarding the Humboldt County display in the Capitol Building in Sacramento,
the members of the Tourism Development Advisory Board feel that such an
important project as the redesigning of the display should be left to the
capable and artistic hands of a professional designer.

After the remodeling of the Capitol Building is complete, there is expected
to be a definite increase in the volume of tourists passing through and viewing
our display. You will agree that the more dynamic and sophisticated our
display is, the better able it will be to compete with the displays of the
larger counties and shine through as being the most worthy of the tourists
vacations (and dollars!).

At the August 10 TDAB meeting the taking of bids for the project was carefully
considered----however, we have a professional designer right in our own midst.
Nelson Richardson has traveled to Sacramento to look at and repair that very
display a short time ago and is willing to devote the time and energy in
redesigning it. He also is very familiar with the beauty and attractions of
Humboldt County and knows his resources.

The Advisory Board, therefore, respectfully requests that Nelson Richardson
be contacted by you and authorized to pursue the redesigning of the display: The
final design to be approved by the Board of Supervisors.


Sincerely,
Ann Boyer
Ann Boyer for the Tourism Development Advisory Board

TDAB/ab

Attch: business card

TOURISM DEVELOPMENT ADVISORY BOARD

COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT

EUREKA, CALIFORNIA 95501          PHONE (707) 445-7418


June 10, 1977

Board of Supervisors
825 Fifth St.
Eureka, CA

Board of Supervisors,

On June 3, 1977, I accompanied Supervisors Renner and Dorsey to Sacramento.
The purpose of my trip was to review and repair the Humboldt County display
in the Capitol building. The following is my report and recommendation.

The display is primarily composed of photographic natural scenes taken
within the county. Its placid content and balanced composition make
it noticeably "quiet" in comparison to other counties. It does not
attract attention because of its inherent lack of structural contrast.
The eye tends to wander aimlessly over its static composition seeking
an outstanding component to look at. This ordered compositional format
also projects a "dated" look that is not in keeping with the rest of the
displays.

The display has a few physical draw backs as well. The heat generated
by the lighting breaks down the adhesive securing the photos. This
creates a maintenance problem and is a bad reflection of the county
when a photo falls out of place. (The building maintenance personnel
are only required to see that the lights are on and the windows are clean.)
The photos are transparencies and color fading is another problem apparent
in all the exhibits utilizing them.

We need an exhibit that will draw the attention of the State Capitol
visitors. I am sure a great number of domestic and foreign tourists
view the displays which have potential bearing on travel decisions.
This was verified by one of the tour guides who said that visitors look
for location maps in the interesting displays. The county should present
a compelling presentation to draw the tourist to our remote part of the
state. The displays are constantly compared to each other as a focal point
within the Capitol complex. A new dynamic presentation of what this
county has to offer could create additional tourist interest, especially
now as the Capitol building prepares to unveil its new construction.
(Six new displays have been installed this year.) I suggest that the
county engage a professional design service to create the best possible
image for our presentation to the public. I would also suggest that
this is not the place to utilize a class room project or group display
that always seems to project a fragmented, crafty look.

Page two.........


This may be the only image of Humboldt County that some people ever see.
Why not make it the best?


Yours very truly,

[signature]

Nelson Richardson
Art Director

NR/ab

cc: Board of Supervisors
    Tourism Board Members

COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT
Inter-Office Memo


DATE;    May 17, 1977

TO:      Members of the Board of Supervisors

FROM:    R. S. Holden, County Administrative Officer

SUBJECT: Replacement of County's Capitol Exhibit

     Approximately three weeks ago, I received a call from Mr. Nelson
Richardson who is associated with the County's Tourism Development
Advisory Board.  In essence, Carl Schillinger had spoken to Mr.
Richardson concerning the repair/replacement of the County's exhibit
at the State Capitol.  Mr. Richardson is employed by Winzler and
Kelly and has an artistic background.

     Mr. Richardson is willing to assist in the prepare of the exhibit.
At the time I spoke with him, he was intending to combine the
replacement of the exhibit with his vacation.  This is now not likely
to be a possibility, and it will require a special effort to gain Mr.
Richardson's assistance in this effort.

     The purpose of this memorandum is merely to inform the Board of this
contact and to request that you alert me as to when Board members will
be traveling to Sacramento.  If sufficient lead time is provided, then
perhaps it will be possible to have Mr. Richardson also journey to
Sacramento to examine the exhibit and then to do the work necessary to
prepare a replacement.

     I am, by means of copies of this memorandum, informing Mrs. Schillinger
and Mr. Richardson that Mr. Richardson may be requested to journey to
Sacramento at just about any time.  Mr. Richardson's phone number is
443-8326.

     Please contact me if you have questions or wish to discuss the contents
of this memorandum.


                                        R.S. Holden
                                        R. S. HOLDEN
                                        County Administrative Officer

RSH/pjs
cc:  Mrs. Carol Schillinger
     Mr. Nelson Richardson
                                        RECEIVED
                                        MAY 19 1977

                                        WINZLER & KELLY
                                        CONSULTING ENGINEERS

COUNTY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE

COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT

EUREKA, CALIFORNIA 95501    PHONE (707) 445-7266


June 2, 1977


Building Manager
State Capitol Building
Room B112
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Sir:

Please accept this letter as formal authorization from the
Humboldt County Board of Supervisors for Mr. Nelson Richardson
to examine and repair/replace the County's exhibit at the State
Capitol Building.

Mr. Dave Zebo was previously authorized to do this work for the
County, but is retiring very soon.

The Board is anxious to have the exhibit reconditioned as soon
as possible, and Mr. Richardson will probably be making more
than one trip to Sacramento to accomplish this.  If the services
of your office are required to undertake this project, you have
the Board's sincere appreciation and thanks for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

R. S. HOLDEN
County Administrative Officer

/lj

COUNTY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE

COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT
EUREKA, CALIFORNIA 95501    PHONE (707) 445-7266

March 30, 1977


Mrs. Carol Schillinger
1460 Pickle Hill Road
Arcata, California    95521

Dear Carol:

     SUBJECT:  State Capitol Window Display

     During its session of March 15, 1977, the Board received the
attached Los Angeles County Fair Association letter and referred
it to the Tourism Development Advisory Board for its recommendation.  A bi-product of the discussion of this letter were remarks
concerning the condition of Humboldt County's Window Display at
the State Capitol.  In essence, it has fallen into a state of
disrepair.

     The Board thought that you might be interested in accepting the
chore to repair the window if the necessary travel and materials
arrangements can be made.  My understanding is that special arrangements have to be made with the State Capitol Maintenance Department
prior to even gaining access to the window.

     Your willingness to take on this chore would be sincerely appreciated by the Board, and I am sure that we can make the necessary
arrangements in advance so that you may make necessary personal
arrangements to journey to Sacramento.  The County would, of
course, cover the necessary expenses.

     Your willingness to consider this request is greatly appreciated.
Please contact me if you desire further information.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        R. S. HOLDEN
                                        County Administrative Officer

RSH/pjs
Attachment
cc:  Members of the Board of Supervisors
     Chairman, Tourism Development Advisory Board

sections.conclusion: Accordingly, we conclude that you are not a "public official" within the meaning of Government Code Section 87100 by virtue of your membership on the Tourism Development Advisory Board. Accordingly, the strictures of the Political Reform Act do not prohibit your participation in that capacity.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Nelson Richardson, as an appointed member of the Humboldt County Tourism Development Advisory Board that recommended him for a design contract, face a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when entering into a contract with the County to redesign the State Capitol Building display?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that Richardson is not a public official under Government Code Section 87100 because the Tourism Development Advisory Board is not a decision-making body and Richardson is not an officer, employee, or consultant of the County by virtue of his board membership. However, the FPPC noted that Government Code Section 1090 prohibiting certain contracts where financial interests exist may apply, which is outside FPPC's interpretive authority.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter from Richardson dated November 28, 1977, and FPPC response letter from Kenneth W. Goshorn dated December 30, 1977. Supporting documents include initial proposal and advisory board recommendation letters. Some text is heavily degraded/OCR corrupted, affecting full clarity of certain passages.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Nelson Richardson, as an appointed member of the Humboldt County Tourism Development Advisory Board that recommended him for a design contract, face a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when entering into a contract with the County to redesign the State Capitol Building display?

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, we conclude that you are not a "public official" within the meaning of Government Code Section 87100 by virtue of your membership on the Tourism Development Advisory Board. Accordingly, the strictures of the Political Reform Act do not prohibit your participation in that capacity.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to an inquiry regarding whether Nelson Richardson, a graphic designer and member of Humboldt County's Tourism Development Advisory Board, faces a conflict of interest in accepting a design contract from the County for the State Capitol Building display that the Advisory Board recommended to him. The FPPC concluded Richardson is not subject to Political Reform Act restrictions as he is not a public official, though other statutory prohibitions may apply.
